title: Functions and lambdas should not reference variables defined in enclosing loops
description: Functions or lambdas should not reference variables defined in enclosing loops.
message: A function or lambda is referring a variable defined in an enclosing loop.
recommendation: Avoid capturing loop variables in function or lambda.

references.name: SonarQube
references.description: Official SonarQube page for this rule.

noncompliant.code: def f(): for i in range(5): def g(): nonlocal i print(i) g() f()
noncompliant.description: A function g references i, which is defined in an enclosing loop.

compliant.code: def f(): for i in range(5): pass
compliant.description: The function does not reference any variable defined in an enclosing loop.

why_is_this_an_issue: References to loop variables within functions or lambdas can result in unintended behavior due to the function or lambda capturing the variable during the loop's execution, potentially leading to inconsistent results.
how_to_fix: Avoid capturing loop variables in function or lambda definitions.

metadata.title: Functions and lambdas should not reference variables defined in enclosing loops
